description:
This huge ringed circle appeared
high on Stone Pitt Hill, a location very remote from any sightseers.  Its
quiet position meant that very few individuals managed to enter the circle,
which preserved its overall condition.  As you cam see from Colin's aerial
image, a number of grapeshots scattered themselves across the diameter of the
circle and rings. A ground survey has not yet been obtained, but there were 36
in number, varying in sizes.  The circumference of the entire circle was
1000ft, which was the largest recorded in that year.
title_raw: Stone Pitt Hill, nr Blackland, Wiltshire. Reported 2nd June.

wheat. The usual feature on the head of the circle was that the flow of the crop was
offset from the circumference of the circle at its starting position. A 1/4 a mile down
the A3 road, another single circle appeared.

description:
A group of scrolls appeared on the top of
Lurkley Hill notorious for circles in the last five years. They were set in a triangle
pattern with each tail facing away from the centre. You could either read them as three
sixes, or three nines. Many people felt quite uneasy in them, like they were emanating a
negative atmosphere. I personally had a bad feeling about them.

junction which led to the village of West Overton. Many Grapeshot circles accompanied the
ring, situated randomly around it, some of them were only half a metre in diameter.

This one was taken at the junction of the A4 and The Avenue, during the summer of 1993.The image above was taken by Colin Andrews, and is one of many
photographs he is currently analysing for anomalous objects captured on film.
Take a closer look in the far right hand side of the image.  There appears
to be a small classic ball of light, which in reality would be quite large, much
larger than a football. probably more like a beach ball in size.  Its
another fascinating image, which could move us more towards an interaction state
with the phenomenon.

description:
A very remote location for this formation.
Set high up on Monkton Down, atleast one mile from Avebury village, this Rape Seed
formation was extremely hard to visit, not only because of its lonely location, but the
crop itself was 6/7ft in places, making it almost impossible to enter the field. This shot
was taken from the field boundary, the small dark patches you see at the top of the field
are actually Tumuli's (ancient burial mounds). The formation consisted of a large and
smaller circle set within an outer ring, with smaller grapeshot circles (two in number)
below the formation.

it's quite impressive in it's construction, especially the wheel feature. The floor
pattern was rather a disappointment, as there was an alarming amount of broken stems

description:
A sensational asteroid belt design, the largest to
appeared in the area. It contained a belt feature comprising of sixty five circles of
various sizes encircling the inner planetary system. This inner design had four very thin
rings, three of them contain connected circles located at different positions as if
representing orbits around the centre circle. The rings were formed with offset positions
to each other resulting in a solar system design. Formed 26th June.

someone will fly over it and take a photograph of it. The whole formation is about 80ft in
diameter. The part of the formation I found most impressive is the heart A shaped
signature 28 or grapeshot 29 just below it. I have never seen this before.

diameter. There was a stream at the bottom of the field.

description:
Many unusual features have been spotted within
the fields, which have been mistaken for the real phenomenon. I was a victim of such a
case when I drove with a friend some 210 miles to find what would have been one of the
first crop circles to appear in 1991. Unfortunately, when we arrived at the location the
circle turned out to be a circular patch within a Rape field, for a cattle trough. A
lesson well learnt, but some reports are correct in the assumptions, and maybe The Giants
trail is indeed part of the real phenomenon, which was first reported in mid August.
title_raw: THE GIANTS TRAIL, The Ridgeway, Marlborough Downs, Nr Marlborough, Wiltshire.

description:
There are currently crop circles in a field near Earlswood
(I'm not sure if it is exactly in Hereford and Worcester, or Solihull district), but they
can be clearly seen from the train between Birmingham and Stratford-upon-Avon, between
Earlswood and Wythall Stations. To get there by foot, go to Tanners Green, and down the
track to the left of the golf club. Go straight across the golf course (it is a public
path), and when you meet a track at right-angles, go right. The crop circles are in the
field on the left.

August 8th, below Milk Hill at Alton Barnes, 200 feet,
wheat. Very Similar to the earlier Silbury Hill formation, except the interior of this one
has standing crop with an inward-facing fractal pattern. And, I estimate there are 204
circles - a new record. It was discovered byNick Rileyat 6.00 AM.
title_raw: A Crop Circle at Milk Hill, Nr Alton Barnes, Wiltshire. Reported 8th August.

description:
Just when you thought it was safe to leave Etchilhampton
for 1997, a new formation appeared directly in the next field along from the Hill. Placed

circle were throw backs to the early formations from 1990 onwards.
title_raw: Crop Circles of 1997 Etchilhampton (3), Nr Devizes, Wiltshire. Reported 8th August.

description:
The single circle positioned further into the field, was a
nice example, flowing in a clockwise direction, with an interesting bunch of standing
stems at one particular place in the circle, close to the perimeter. These stems were
located at a point were a seed or drill line ran parallel with the tramlines. This was
worth noting I felt, as they where deliberately left by the source. The floor pattern will
be seen on the forthcoming MPEG, which you will be able to watch soon, from this report.

Crop Circles of 1997 Etchilhampton (4), Nr Devizes, Wiltshire. Reported 8tht

who reports that the circle centres were laid in three blocks swirled out clockwise with

entry and the tramlines were very overgrown with many cobwebs. There were swollen nodes
and blown, blackened nodes. There were also some double node bends especially noticeable
on the gap seeking stems. The corn was very tightly laid in blocks, very springy
underfoot, and there
title_raw: Crop Circles of 1997 Sevington, Nr Ashford, Kent. Reported 11th August.

location:

    Field Car Design, nr Alton Barnes, Wiltshire
description:
This is a side of the Crop Circle phenomenon,
which seems to be becoming increasingly more apparent in the last couple of years. Many
companies are recognising the power of the phenomenon to advertise their products.Mitsubishi,the Japanese car manufactures are the latest to commission a group of 
artists to promote their latest model. The location for this design was what I call
"The Sanctuary" for the phenomenon East Field/Alton Barnes area. A number people
felt it was inappropriate for the Crop Circle phenomenon and was not complimentary towards
other designs we have experienced this season.
title_raw: East Field Car Design (Mitsubishi), nr Alton Barnes, Wiltshire. Reported 5th August

description:
This remarkable, 400 feet formation arrived
6th August near Lockeridge in the  same wheat field as the "Hydra." She features lays that swirl in different
directions within the big circles, which when seen from the air, create additional,
gleaming geometry.  That, together with "plated" crop and a little circle
with it's stems bent halfway down, makes her clearly a member of a rapidly evolving family
of formations that began this year with the "Dragon Larva,"
the "Cat's Eyes," and most recently, the "Stingray".
title_raw: Lockeridge (3), Nr Avebury, Wiltshire.  Reported 6th August

location:

	August
description:
it was a very strange formation even more strange 
			was the height of the crop, even if it was already ripe and dry the 
			ears only reached lower that our knees. the overall design was kind 
			of irregular and the lay pretty rough, however, hard to tell because 
			the flattened plants were so short. on the ground there was nearly 
			no contrast between standing and flattened crop visible.Andreas Müller
title_raw:
Crop Circle at Old Sarum, nr Sailsbury, Wiltshire. Reported 6th August

description:
The splendid Pentagram series continues with
this 200 feet formation in wheat, August 8th, in the same field south of Beckhampton,
Wiltshire, where the "StingRay" is.  The
first of the series was back in 1993, at Bythorn.  Then there were none until last
year, when we received the "Hopi Star," and now we have hadthreethis
summer - so far!
title_raw: Beckhampton (4), nr Avebury, Wiltshire. Reported 8th August

1997 Milk Hill formation.
title_raw: Tawsmead Copse, Nr West Stowell,Wiltshire. Formed 9th  August.

description:
It's located in a field at Yatesbury across
from the previous circle. It's in the same field as the Meteor hanger.It appears to be a pyramid, with three small circles at the apex, rather like a version of
a Freemason's symbol. From what I could tell, only a few people have been in the circle
besides my son and myself. We did encounter a car with three people inside, who were also
carrying a lot of corn from the circle. It was neatly laid out but there were quite a few
standing stems peppering the place.
title_raw: Yatesbury, nr Avebury, Wiltshire. Reported 10th August

    Common, Stourport on Severn, nr Kidderminister, Herefordshire
description:
These formations are south west of Stourport
on Severn just beyond the small estate known as Areley Common. The circle was formed on
the 16/17th August 1998 within the centre of its field on a gentle gradient. The circle
itself is clockwise with a good lay. several hundred metres to the south east of the
circle lies a small grapeshot approx. 1m in diameter swirled clockwise. The northern edge
of the circle has a straight edge to it as it avoids running into the adjacent tramline.
title_raw:
Areley Common, Stourport on Severn, nr Kidderminister, Herefrodshire. Reported 17th
August

    Hill, nr Broad Hinton, Wiltshire
description:
The Koch "Snowflake"  returns
in an utterly unforeseen form! August 18th north of Avebury in the vicinity of Hackpen
Hill, near where another member of the family  (The Strange
Attractor) appeared in the same field last summer,
which was the final formation of that year. This one is about 150 feet from corner to
corner. The three arms have no self-similar branches, so it's not technically a fractal.
title_raw: Hackpen Hill, nr Broad Hinton, Wiltshire. Reported 17th August
Horsebridge, nr Hailsham, East Sussex. Reported 17th August.

    17th August
description:
The first formation in Linseed (flax) in
years appeared at Stanton St. Bernard on August 17th. (Sadly, the blue flowers were
already gone!) It consists of two triangles (shown by grey outlines) that were precisely
laid, surrounded by many   reasonably good looking circles, and a lot ofjunk.
Perhaps the "Ear" and "UFO" were added by visitors - or it's all a
hoax.
title_raw: Stanton St Bernard, nr Alton Barnes, Wiltshire. Reported 17th August

This handsome 300 footer appeared August 19th
1998 near Avebury "Sanctuary" on the A4, in wheat.  It is obviously similar
to the first formation last year, the "Spinning Crescents,"
below Barbury Castle.
title_raw: The Sanctuary, nr West Overton, Wiltshire.  Reported 19th August
All Cannings, nr Devizes, Wiltshire. Reported 21st August

description:
This formation was discovered on
Monday morning in the field opposite the Avebury coach/car park. It is near the top of the
slope over the brow from theprevious flower formationwhich appeared at the beginning
of July. The large circle contains an eight fold geometry star constructed from
intersecting lines subdividing the outline of a square, and the crop is mainly swept down
in one direction. There are several small accompanying grapeshots with intricate nested
centres.
title_raw: Avebury, nr Marlborough, Wiltshire.  Reported 24th  August

description:
I discovered this formation on the evening of
31st August 1998, together with my friend Peter Ainsworth. It is located in the same field
as the 21st June 'Cat's Eye' formation, and is situated to the right of that formation,
between it and the next set of tramlines. We were unable to spend long in the formation
because it was getting dark, though we did a basic examination and took some photographs.The new formation is sited parallel to the tramlines and consists of two small circles,
one larger than the other, joined by a path. Midway up the path is a 'bar', which extends
on either side. Betw

    Lane Estate,  Nr Rothwell, Leeds. Yorkshire
description:
Just for information I attach a jpg file containing a
cutting from our local paper in Rothwell, Leeds.As far as I'm aware no crop circles have been reported in this area before.I viewed the formation myself this morning (17th September). Crops were being harvested in
neighbouring fields so I don't think it will be there  much longer.The formation is close to a residential area and having viewed it myself I am convinced
that this is not the work of untrained pranksters.Reported byDave Readman.
title_raw: Wood Lane Estate,  Nr Rothwell, Leeds. Yorkshire. Reported 17th September.

description:
Fields of mature rye (?) were noticed in Norfolk back in 
   December 2003 at Marsham, Aylsham and Frettenham (the first two locations 
   having hosted crop circles previously). This 6 - 8 ft (radius) clockwise 
   circle was seen in the field at Aylsham at the end of January. By the time 
   the photos were taken the crop had been fairly battered by rain and snow. 
   Several areas of the field had also suffered rook and rabbit damage. A sign 
   of things to come in Norfolk this year?Reported by John Sayer
title_raw: Crop Circle at Aylsham, Norfolk, December 2003
